title: dianthus humilis
intro: Dianthus humilis, also known as the dwarf pink or low pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic qualities. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Dianthus humilis can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and offer relief from various health issues.
